title: LINE Pay Online API v3
overview: The LINE Pay Online API is a web API used for processing online payments; all communication occurs over HTTPS. Requests are sent to https://{host}/{apiPath}?{queryString}. Common request headers include Content-Type: application/json, X-LINE-Authorization (HMAC-SHA256 credentials), X-LINE-Authorization-Nonce (a random UUID v1/v4 or timestamp), and X-LINE-ChannelId. API responses always return HTTP 200, with a JSON body of {returnCode, returnMessage, info}. NOTE: this case was extracted from the single online-api-v3 overview page (common conventions, headers, base URLs, result codes, and the Request body example); the per-endpoint request/response field tables (Confirm/Capture/Void/Refund/Payment Details/Check) live on JavaScript-rendered sub-pages that were not captured and are recorded in missing.

security_schemes.name: X-LINE-Authorization (HMAC-SHA256)
security_schemes.details: Credentials generated by the HMAC SHA256 algorithm, sent in the X-LINE-Authorization header. Used together with X-LINE-Authorization-Nonce (a randomly generated UUID v1/v4 or timestamp) and X-LINE-ChannelId (Channel ID). The exact HMAC payload assembly (channel secret + URI + body + nonce) is described on the Prerequisites page, not in this source.

security_schemes.name: X-LINE-ChannelId
security_schemes.details: Channel ID, required on every request.

endpoints.summary: Request API. Reserve a payment; returns a transactionId and (for web) a payment URL the customer is redirected to for LINE Pay authentication.
Confirm API. Confirm (capture) a reserved payment after the customer completes LINE Pay authentication (returnCode 0110).
Capture API. Capture a previously authorized payment.
Void API. Void a previously authorized (not yet captured) payment.
Refund API. Refund a completed payment (full or partial).
Payment Details API. Retrieve payment/transaction details by transactionId or orderId.
Check Payment Request Status API. Check whether the customer has completed LINE Pay authentication for a payment request.

schemas.name: Response Envelope

fields.name: returnCode
fields.description: Result code (see Result code table). Always included.

fields.name: returnMessage
fields.description: Message corresponding to returnCode. Always included.

fields.name: info
fields.description: API request result data. Included only when the API call is successful (Conditional).

fields.name: info.orderId
fields.description: Merchant order ID (from the success response example).

fields.name: info.transactionId
fields.description: 19-digit transaction ID (process as 64-bit long; convert to string if needed).

fields.name: info.payInfo[].method
fields.description: Payment method, e.g. BALANCE (from the success response example).

fields.name: info.payInfo[].amount
fields.description: Amount for the payment method (from the success response example).

schemas.constraints: API responses always return HTTP 200; the application-level outcome is in returnCode. On failure the info field is omitted.
schemas.source: Response body table + Success/failure response examples

schemas.name: Payment Request Body

fields.name: productName
fields.description: Product name (from the Request body example).

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Payment amount (from the Request body example).

fields.name: currency
fields.description: Currency code, e.g. TWD (from the Request body example).

fields.name: orderId
fields.description: Merchant order ID (from the Request body example).

schemas.constraints: Only the four fields shown in the overview's Request body example are captured; the full v3 Request schema (packages[], redirectUrls{confirmUrl,cancelUrl}, options, etc.) is on the JS-rendered Request endpoint sub-page and is not in this source.

errors.meaning: Request successful. For a payment request status check, indicates the customer has not yet completed LINE Pay authentication.
The customer has completed LINE Pay authentication; you can proceed with payment confirmation.
The customer canceled the payment, or the LINE Pay authentication waiting time expired.
Payment failed.
Payment completed.
The customer isn't a LINE Pay user.
The customer is currently unable to make transactions with LINE Pay.
The merchant isn't registered on the merchant center. Check credentials.
LINE Pay is currently unavailable for the merchant.
There is an error in the request header information.
The credit card cannot be used.
There is an error in the amount information.
There is a problem with the account status (EPI not enabled, or pre-approved key discarded).
The balance is insufficient.
Payment is in progress.
There are no transaction details.
There is a duplicate transaction.
The payment request amount and the capture amount differ.
This is an invalid transaction ID.
There is no payment request information.
Refund isn't available (refund period has expired).
Exceeded the refundable amount.
The transaction has already been refunded.
Transaction details with the same order ID already exist.
Exceeded the maximum number of transactions that can be retrieved (100).
The currency isn't supported by the merchant.
API request is duplicated.
Internal error occurred during the request.
Temporary error occurred. Please try again later.
Parameter error occurred.
JSON data format error occurred.

operational.detail: Every request requires Content-Type: application/json, X-LINE-Authorization (HMAC-SHA256), X-LINE-Authorization-Nonce (random UUID v1/v4 or timestamp), and X-LINE-ChannelId. Credential preparation is on the Prerequisites page.
API responses always return HTTP 200; the actual outcome is carried in the body returnCode (0000 = success).

Transaction ID is a 19-digit integer identifying an action (request/confirm/capture/refund). Process it as a 64-bit long integer; if a system cannot, convert to string to avoid precision loss (values exceed JS MAX_SAFE_INTEGER 2^53-1). A handleBigInteger helper wraps 16+ digit sequences in quotes before JSON.parse.
To enable LINE Pay online payments you must also implement redirection pages that act as the interface between your service and the LINE Pay server.

The documented failure example uses fields {resultCode, statusMessage} (e.g. 1104, 'Merchant not found.'), which differs from the standard {returnCode, returnMessage} envelope — recorded verbatim from the source.

missing: Per-endpoint request/response field tables for Confirm, Capture, Void, Refund, Payment Details, and Check Payment Request Status are not in the captured overview page (they are on JavaScript-rendered sub-pages); only the endpoint paths are known.
The full v3 Payment Request schema (packages[], redirectUrls{confirmUrl,cancelUrl}, options, etc.) is not in this source — only the four-field example (productName/amount/currency/orderId).
The exact HMAC-SHA256 payload assembly (channel secret + URI + request body + nonce) is on the Prerequisites page, not in this source.
Query/path parameters for GET /v3/payments and the check endpoint (e.g. transactionId, orderId, fields) are not enumerated in this source.
The complete Result code table is larger than captured; only a representative subset (including credit-card codes 1280-1298) was recorded — the full list is in the source's Result code section.
